title: State, Not Tokens: Repository-Scale Agent Reasoning Is Bound by State Architecture
description: A controlled study showing repository-scale agent reasoning is bound by state architecture, not nominal context length. Under an unforgeable JavaScript→TypeScript migration oracle (strict tsc, immutable tests, zero escape hatches), three arms hold model, tools, scaffold, and oracle constant and vary only how state flows between bounded workers: a single-context monolith, a durable arm that accumulates each completed dependency layer as a committed artifact on a shared tree, and a stateless-RAG arm whose per-file workers retrieve context but never see each other's results. The paper also reports external validation on the third-party NL2Repo-Bench benchmark (91.1% mean test-pass rate, about 2.28x the published ~40% state of the art). Repository includes the paper, the harness, and the canonical trial data.
